feed.title: Combined summary - ASmap dashboard: tracking the asmap-data history against the observed network

entry.title: Combined summary - ASmap dashboard: tracking the asmap-data history against the observed network
entry.summary: The ASmap dashboard, a recent addition to the Summer of Bitcoin project, offers an advanced platform for exploring the history and evolution of bitcoin-core/asmap-data. Available online at [this link](https://jorisstrakeljahn.github.io/asmap-dashboard/), the dashboard allows users to interactively trace changes in address space allocations among different operators with each software release. It also illustrates the growth of entry counts and operator coverage through time. A standout feature is its diff explorer, which provides detailed analyses of alterations between any two builds. In terms of functionality, the Network tab on the dashboard introduces a unique method to assess the .dat files by applying a scoring system that evaluates each build against actually observed nodes. This approach not only helps in understanding how peers are grouped during actual node interactions but also in monitoring the scoring's degradation over time. Such insights are crucial for assessing the practical utility of each build in real scenarios. The developers encourage user feedback on various aspects including the relevance of metrics, clarity of data representation, and suggestions for additional metrics or views. Contributions toward identifying bugs are also welcomed. A notable discussion involves enhancing the dashboard’s ability to track significant ASN movements within the network. A proposed improvement for the dashboard's "Top Movers table" suggests adding a recurrence indicator for each move across ASNs, distinguishing routine internal shifts from unusual or noteworthy ones. This could involve counting how frequently each move appears in historical release steps to identify if a move is commonplace or exceptional. The idea is to filter and highlight large and infrequent moves as potentially interesting anomalies, providing deeper insights into the dynamics of network changes. This enhancement aims to refine the analytical capabilities of the ASmap dashboard, making it a more valuable tool for researchers and analysts dealing with Bitcoin network data.
